content.full_text:
September 24, 1991

California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Attention: Mark R. Soble

Re: FPPC File #91/244
Citizens Voice

Dear Mr. Soble:

Thank you for your letter of September 5, 1991.

I would like to clarify one item in your Disposition Sheet. Why would our newsletter, if published closer to an election, constitute express advocacy?

The newsletter is written to report on the actions of elected officials not candidates. If we stick to that purpose then I fail to see how this will constitute advocacy.

One of the current council members has indicated he will stand for election in 1992. If we report his campaign promise against his actions would that constitute advocacy? I assume it would and therefore we would report only the actions of the incumbent and not the candidate.

The purpose of this newsletter is to motivate people to vote in the upcoming city council election, and not to support or oppose candidates or issues.

Your response and advice is appreciated.

Sincerely,

John D. Stiles, Jr.
President/Broker

JDS:cvh
